description: Send the signal anywhere in HISE!

parameterGroups.label: Gain
parameterGroups.rationale: Controls the amplitude of the signal pushed to the global signal slot.

properties.note: String ID of the global signal slot. Only one global_send can be connected to each slot. The slot is created on demand in the GlobalRoutingManager.

gaps.question: Does global_send copy the audio to the shared buffer (signal continues in the local chain) or consume it (silence after the send)?
gaps.context: From infrastructure: push() uses copyWithMultiply into shared buffer. But does the local signal path continue with the original signal unmodified?

gaps.question: The Value parameter (0-1) is used as the gain multiplier in push(). Is this linear gain or does it use any curve/conversion?
gaps.context: From infrastructure: push uses FloatVectorOperations::copyWithMultiply with the value directly. Confirm it is linear.

gaps.question: The node is marked UncompileableNode. What is the architectural reason it cannot be compiled to C++?
gaps.context: Users doing C++ export need to know this limitation and what alternative to use (routing.send/receive or routing.global_cable for control values).

gaps.question: From infrastructure: only one send node per signal slot. What error/behaviour occurs if a second global_send tries to connect to an occupied slot?
gaps.context: Users need to understand this constraint to avoid debugging confusion.

notes: routing.global_send pushes audio to a global signal slot that routing.global_receive nodes read from. Marked UncompileableNode -- cannot be used in C++ exported networks. Value parameter acts as a gain multiplier. Only one sender per slot but multiple receivers allowed. The description 'Send the signal anywhere in HISE!' is shared with global_receive (copy-paste error in base data) -- global_receive should say 'Receive' instead.
